trigger.reason: Apple's WWDC 2026 announcements include multiple new AI-powered product releases (Siri AI overhaul, Image Playground improvements, Photos editing features, Shortcuts AI workflow builder, new dedicated Siri app) that constitute material capability demos and product releases likely to trigger sympathy-fade selling in AI-adjacent sectors (low-code/no-code tools, customer-service software, photo editing software, automation platforms).

primary_event.headline: Apple's long-awaited AI Siri overhaul is finally here, with new Image Playground, Photos AI editing, and Shortcuts AI workflow builder
primary_event.summary: Apple announced a comprehensive AI upgrade at WWDC 2026, including a redesigned Siri AI assistant with expanded capabilities, improvements to Image Playground generative imaging, new AI-powered photo editing features (Reframe), and an AI-driven Shortcuts app for workflow automation. The company also introduced a dedicated Siri app and lowered API costs for small developers.

secondary_events.headline: Apple will let you build workflows using AI in its new Shortcuts app
Apple's Image Playground doesn't suck anymore
Apple's Photos app is getting new AI editing features

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: Apple WWDC 2026 AI announcements (Siri overhaul, Image Playground, Photos AI editing, Shortcuts AI workflow builder) triggering sympathy-fade across AI-adjacent sectors.
discovery.run_summary: Apple's WWDC AI announcements are a consumer-facing capability demo with limited direct overlap to most curated candidates. The biggest sympathy moves are in healthcare analytics (HCAT down sharply) and edtech (LRN modestly down), neither of which has meaningful business-model overlap with Siri/Image Playground/Shortcuts. Semi names (AMBA, ONTO, AEIS) actually rallied — these are AI beneficiaries, not victims, and don't need a sympathy-fade thesis today.

discoveries.name: Health Catalyst, Inc
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors name competition from EHR companies (Epic, Oracle Health), Optum, and IBM — not consumer AI assistants. The only AI mention is general 'content we obtain from third parties or AI resources' language. No exposure to Siri/Image Playground/Shortcuts workflow automation.
discoveries.thesis: HCAT sells provider-side data warehousing and analytics that require HIPAA BAAs, EHR integration (Epic/Oracle), and multi-year implementations. Apple's consumer AI announcements have zero overlap with hospital analytics workflows. However, HCAT has real company-specific problems (Project Nexus restructuring, DOS-to-Ignite migration churn, repeated workforce reductions) that may be the actual driver of the sharp drop — not Apple. The sympathy-fade thesis is clean from a filings standpoint, but the move's magnitude likely reflects company-specific issues rather than pure WWDC reaction.
discoveries.what_confirms: Bounce-back within 2-3 sessions as Apple news cycle fades; no analyst notes tying HCAT weakness to Apple announcements; stable Ignite migration commentary on next call.
discoveries.what_kills: If the drop is actually about Project Nexus / Ignite migration churn (filings flag both as live issues), the move is justified on company-specific grounds and won't recover. The five-day move being sharply positive but today negative suggests profit-taking on a name with real operational stress, not Apple sympathy.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Healthcare analytics names get caught in generic AI sympathy waves despite HIPAA/EHR moats. But always check for concurrent operating stress (restructuring plans) before calling unjustified — a name can have both a clean filings story AND real problems.
discoveries.catalyst: Apple's long-awaited AI Siri overhaul is finally here, with new Image Playground, Photos AI editing, and Shortcuts AI workflow builder
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: HCAT is healthcare analytics software, loosely fitting the 'workflow automation / data agents' panic narrative from WWDC's Shortcuts AI announcement.
research_pointers: Check if Project Nexus restructuring details or Ignite migration churn news hit today separately from Apple
Look at Q1 2026 dollar-based retention rate trajectory
Confirm whether five-day +25% move was an earnings/guide bounce that is now being faded
Compare HCAT move to other healthcare-IT names (VEEV, DOCS) on the same day

discoveries.name: Stride, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors describe revenue dependency on per-pupil state funding, charter authorizations, and ESSA accountability. AI is mentioned only as a peripheral risk ('AI technology is new and developing... compliance, reputational challenges'). Core revenue model is school-as-a-service contracts with accredited charter schools — a credentialing/funding moat that consumer Siri features do not touch.
discoveries.thesis: LRN's revenue is per-pupil state funding flowing through accredited K-12 charter contracts. The legal/regulatory wrapper (state charters, ESSA compliance, accredited transcripts) is the product. Apple shipping a better Siri or Image Playground does not let a family enroll their child or obtain a state-recognized diploma. The modest negative move is a generic edtech reflex, not a filings-supported threat.
discoveries.what_confirms: Sell-side notes pointing out that Stride is structurally different from consumer edtech (DUOL/CHGG); stable enrollment pre-announcements for the next school year.
discoveries.what_kills: A state AG or governor citing AI in proposing virtual-school funding cuts; charter non-renewals tied to AI-tutor alternatives — neither in filings or news today.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Accredited K-12 (LRN) and consumer language apps (DUOL) get lumped together in 'edtech' sympathy waves but have totally different moats. Funding-eligibility and accreditation are not capabilities Apple can ship at WWDC.
discoveries.catalyst: Apple's long-awaited AI Siri overhaul is finally here, with new Image Playground, Photos AI editing, and Shortcuts AI workflow builder
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Edtech is on the curated AI-vulnerable list and LRN trades on AI tutor narratives despite the accreditation moat.
research_pointers: Confirm no concurrent state-level charter or funding news today
Track LRN vs CHGG/DUOL relative performance to isolate the accreditation premium
Review upcoming back-to-school enrollment data for any AI-tutor cannibalization signal

discoveries.name: Verra Mobility Corporation
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors describe physical camera infrastructure, multi-year municipal contracts (NYCDOT 5-yr, ~15% of revenue), court-admissible photo enforcement, and customer concentration. AI mentioned generically as both opportunity and risk; no language tying Siri/Image Playground/Shortcuts to revenue threat. However, Q1 risk factor flags Commercial Services renewal risk with a >10% customer — a real concurrent overhang.
discoveries.thesis: Verra's product is municipal contracts plus physical cameras with chain-of-custody evidence. Apple's consumer AI does not generate court-admissible violation evidence or hold municipal contracts. The modest drop on the WWDC day is generic 'workflow automation' sympathy with no filings support. Caveat: the five-day move is sharply negative, suggesting the market is already pricing the Commercial Services renewal uncertainty disclosed in the 10-Q — that overhang is real and separate from any AI sympathy.
discoveries.what_confirms: Successful Commercial Services renewal disclosure; no new ballot initiatives against photo enforcement; bounce as AI news cycle fades.
discoveries.what_kills: The Commercial Services contract negotiation (a known overhang in the Q) producing materially worse pricing/terms — this would justify the multi-day weakness independent of Apple.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Physical-world municipal services rarely belong in AI sympathy baskets; the legal evidentiary moat is durable. But check for concurrent customer-concentration disclosures before calling the move pure sympathy.
discoveries.catalyst: Apple's long-awaited AI Siri overhaul is finally here, with new Image Playground, Photos AI editing, and Shortcuts AI workflow builder
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Verra is loosely in the 'workflow automation' bucket from WWDC's Shortcuts AI panic narrative.
research_pointers: Identify the Commercial Services customer in renewal negotiations and timing
Confirm extended week-long weakness is contract-renewal-driven, not Apple-driven
Check NYCDOT performance under new contract terms (service credits, liquidated damages)

skipped.reason: Stock rallied sharply (positive move, positive 5-day) — no sympathy-fade to capture; sympathy-fade screen requires a negative move.
Stock rallied sharply on WWDC day — no negative sympathy reaction to fade. The expected 'edtech panic' narrative did not fire.
Positive move — Ambarella is being treated as an edge-AI beneficiary by the tape today, consistent with curation. No sympathy-fade BUY to call.
Positive move — semi process control treated as AI capex beneficiary, not victim. No sympathy fade to fade.
Positive move — power delivery for fabs/data centers seen as AI buildout beneficiary. No negative reaction to grade.
